data.body:
## Done Definitions

- When this epic is complete, fix-bug Step 1 classifies bugs as llm-behavioral when ticket content references agent/skill names or prompt behavior, OR when all affected files are .md skill/agent/prompt files, and routes to dso:bot-psychologist
  ← Satisfies: SC1 "fix-bug Step 1 gains an llm-behavioral classification branch"
- When this epic is complete, plugins/dso/agents/bot-psychologist.md exists with the 15-point failure taxonomy, 5 RCA probe definitions, and KERNEL minimal-fix constraint defined inline
  ← Satisfies: SC2 "dso:bot-psychologist agent definition exists"
- When this epic is complete, the bot-psychologist report uses ROOT_CAUSE, confidence, proposed_fixes, and hypothesis_tests (with verdict field) so fix-bug Step 3.5 Hypothesis Validation Gate works without modification
  ← Satisfies: SC3 "report conforms to fix-bug RESULT schema"
- When this epic is complete, fix-bug llm-behavioral path skips Step 5 (RED test) and Step 5.5 (RED-before-fix gate) while Step 3.5 and Step 4 still apply
  ← Satisfies: SC4 "llm-behavioral path skips Step 5 and Step 5.5"
- When this epic is complete, fix-bug HARD-GATE explicitly allows combined investigation + fix-proposal dispatch for llm-behavioral bugs
  ← Satisfies: SC5 "HARD-GATE amended for llm-behavioral"
- When this epic is complete, llm-debugging-agent.md and llm-debugging-research.md (from commit 4af34287) are deleted from the repository
  ← Satisfies: SC6 "research files deleted after completion"
- When this epic is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic
  ← Satisfies: TDD DoD

## Scope

- IN: bot-psychologist agent definition, fix-bug llm-behavioral classification branch, fix-bug HARD-GATE amendment, fix-bug Step 5/5.5 gate exemption for llm-behavioral, agent-routing.conf update, research file cleanup
- OUT: Change execution workflow (user approval, applying fixes) — tracked in separate dependency epic. Standalone /dso:debug-llm skill. Eval framework for agent behavioral testing (separate epic). Modifications to debug-everything dispatch logic (debug-everything routes to fix-bug, which handles classification).

## Considerations

- [Testing] Agent definition tests must use structural assertions (grep for taxonomy items, probe names, RESULT schema fields) — no live LLM calls in CI
- [Testing] fix-bug classification needs mock ticket fixtures (agent/skill name refs vs code-only refs) to test routing correctness
- [Reliability] Nested sub-agent prohibition: fix-bug must read bot-psychologist agent definition inline (same pattern as complexity-evaluator) when running as a sub-agent of debug-everything
- [Reliability] False-positive routing: keyword matching should require behavioral language ("skips," "misinterprets," "drifts") not just agent name mentions to reduce misrouting code bugs
- [Maintainability] agent-routing.conf must map llm_behavioral category to dso:bot-psychologist; naming must be consistent across agent file, routing conf, and SKILL.md references
- [Maintainability] HARD-GATE and Step 5/5.5 amendments must be scoped exclusively to llm-behavioral classification — no accidental gate relaxation for code bugs
